title: Syracuse hires budget director who is expected to stay in role for Mayor-elect Owens
summary: Mayor Walsh appointed Evan Loving as Director of the Office of Management and Budget in coordination with Mayor-elect Sharon Owens, who will assume office in January 2026. Loving, a returning city employee with prior experience as special assistant to Walsh and subsequent roles in Virginia local government and consulting, will lead budget development, capital planning, purchasing, and equity compliance. The position has been vacant since February 2025 when former director Tim Rudd was terminated amid controversy.

political_signal.description: Incoming Mayor Owens actively participated in hiring decision, signaling continuity and alignment on fiscal management priorities. Appointment demonstrates transition coordination between administrations and Owens' stated intent to improve budget development engagement with Common Council.

processing_notes: Department reference corrected from mechanical extraction; article references 'Office of Management and Budget' not 'Department of Finance' or 'Department of Permits and Development'
Mechanical extraction missed key entities: Ben Walsh, Tim Rudd, Julie Castellitto, and Syracuse Common Council
Article contains institutional context on prior budget conflict (2024) and Rudd termination controversy that informs this appointment
Budget cycle timing noted: next major OMB responsibility is April 2026 budget proposal to Common Council
$125,000 salary represents $7,000 increase over predecessor Rudd's $118,000 (2024)
